channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Labour criticised for response to heavy defeat in Gorton and Denton byelection
item.description: Marina Hyde delivers a scathing critique of Labour’s reaction to its poor performance in the Gorton and Denton byelection, where the party fell to third place despite the seat being regarded as a hist...

item.title: Pressure Mounts on Starmer as Labour Faces Backlash Over By-Election Loss
item.description: Sir Keir Starmer is facing growing criticism from within the Labour Party following a poor by-election result in Gorton and Denton. High-profile figures, including former deputy prime minister Angela ...

item.title: EU Project Develops PFAS-Free and Cost-Effective Green Hydrogen Technology
item.description: Green hydrogen is considered a crucial component for reducing reliance on fossil fuels, but current production methods face major challenges. Proton exchange membrane (PEM) electrolysis, one of the le...

item.title: Gorton and Denton by-election sees tight contest amid concerns over family voting
item.description: The Gorton and Denton by-election, a key test of Sir Keir Starmer’s leadership, has entered a tense counting phase following the close of polls. Although Labour secured over 50 per cent in the 2024 ge...

item.title: Ultrahuman Launches Ring Pro With 15-Day Battery Life as It Seeks U.S. Market Reentry After Patent Dispute
item.description: Ultrahuman has introduced the Ring Pro, its third-generation smart ring, as it works to restore its position in the U.S. market following a patent dispute with rival Oura. The Bengaluru-based wearable...

item.title: UK asylum applications fall slightly in 2025 despite rise in Channel crossings
item.description: In 2025, the number of asylum claims in the United Kingdom decreased by 4%, totalling 100,625 applications, according to Home Office data. Despite this overall decline, arrivals via small boats crossi...

item.title: Ian Huntley in Serious Condition Following Attack at HMP Frankland
item.description: Ian Huntley, the convicted murderer of 10-year-olds Holly Wells and Jessica Chapman in Soham in 2002, remains in hospital in a serious condition following an alleged attack by another inmate at HMP Fr...

item.title: A Glimpse into the Luxurious Design of Nanjing's Public Bathroom
item.description: Most public restrooms are functional, but one located inside Deji Plaza in Nanjing, China, has raised the bar to an entirely new level. Designed by Shanghai-based X+Living, this restroom is a stunning...

item.title: CCTV evidence presented in trial over murder of pregnant woman
item.description: The trial of Stephen McCullagh, 36, who is accused of murdering his pregnant ex-partner Natalie McNally, 32, in Lurgan, County Armagh, has entered its fourth day. McNally, 15 weeks pregnant, was found...

item.title: India advances towards a single-dose dengue vaccine covering all four serotypes
item.description: India is progressing towards a significant breakthrough in dengue prevention with Panacea Biotec developing DengiAll, a single-dose vaccine effective against all four dengue serotypes. Dengue infects ...

item.title: Hungarian Prime Minister Claims Ukraine Delaying Druzhba Pipeline, Deploys Soldiers to Protect Energy Sites
item.description: Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán has accused Ukraine of deliberately delaying the reopening of the Druzhba pipeline, a key route for Russian oil to Hungary and Slovakia, calling it an 'oil blocka...

item.title: Real Madrid Advance in Champions League Amid Racism Allegations, Vinicius Scores as Tchouameni Calls Win a Stand Against Racism
item.description: Real Madrid secured their place in the Champions League last 16 with a 2-1 victory over Benfica in the second leg of their knockout play-off tie, resulting in a 3-1 aggregate win. The match was oversh...

item.title: Bloomberg Report on Apple’s Future Touch-Screen MacBook Pro with Dynamic Island
item.description: The provided link intended to direct readers to a Bloomberg news article about Apple’s reported plans for a touch-screen MacBook Pro featuring a Dynamic Island and a redesigned interface. However, the...

item.title: Trump's dismissive joke about US women's Olympic hockey team reveals inconsistencies in his support for women's sports
item.description: Following the United States' victories in both the men's and women's ice hockey events at the 2026 Winter Olympics in Milan-Cortina, President Donald Trump congratulated the men's team via a locker-ro...

item.title: Green Party by-election victory signals further fragmentation of Britain’s traditional two-party system
item.description: The Green Party’s victory in the Gorton and Denton by-election marks a historic breakthrough and underlines the growing volatility of British politics. Hannah Spencer became the first Green candidate ...

item.title: Labour falls to third place as Greens win Gorton and Denton by-election in major upset
item.description: The Green Party has secured a historic victory in the Gorton and Denton by-election, overturning what had long been considered a safe Labour seat and dealing a significant political blow to Prime Mini...

item.title: Belgorod Hit by Ukrainian Missiles as IMF Approves $8.1bn Aid Package for Ukraine
item.description: Russian authorities reported significant damage in the border town of Belgorod following missile strikes on energy facilities, causing disruptions to electricity, water, and heating. This marks the se...

item.title: The Kākāpō: Conservation Efforts to Save New Zealand’s Flightless Parrot
item.description: The Kākāpō, also known as the Owl Parrot, is one of the most unique birds in the world, native to New Zealand’s remote islands. This nocturnal, flightless parrot is the heaviest on Earth, reaching up ...

item.title: Newcastle United beat Qarabag 3-2 to reach Champions League last 16 with 9-3 aggregate win
item.description: Newcastle United progressed into the UEFA Champions League last 16 after defeating Qarabag 3-2 in the second leg of their knockout play-off at St James’ Park on 24 February 2026, securing a 9-3 aggreg...

item.title: Police Sergeant Dismissed for Faking Remote Work Using Laptop Trick
item.description: An Avon and Somerset Police sergeant, identified only as Sergeant X, has been dismissed and banned from law enforcement after admitting to faking her work-from-home hours using a simple trick. During ...
